ASSISTANT REPLY (REG):
Certainly! Here are brief guides for both exercises:
Body Scan:

Find a comfortable position and close your eyes if you like.
Start by focusing on your toes, noticing any sensations, and gradually move up through your body—feet, legs, hips, stomach, chest, arms, hands, neck, and head.
Pause at each area, and if your mind wanders, gently bring your focus back to the sensations.
Sensory Grounding:

Take a deep breath and engage each of your senses: Identify 5 things you can see, 4 things you can touch, 3 things you can hear, 2 things you can smell, and 1 thing you can taste.
Spend a few moments with each sense before moving to the next.
Would you like to try one together sometime to see how it feels?
